6.gl: figuratively, a magistrate; by Hebraism, very

15.gl: figuratively, a magistrate; by Hebraism, very
15.dv: of uncertain affinity; a deity, especially (with G3588 (ὁ)) the supreme Divinity;

23.gl: figuratively, a magistrate; by Hebraism, very
23.dv: of uncertain affinity; a deity, especially (with G3588 (ὁ)) the supreme Divinity;

23.gl: properly, royalty, i.e. (abstractly) rule, or (concretely) a realm (literally or figuratively)

25.gl: sacred (physically, pure, morally blameless or religious, ceremonially, consecrated)
a foreteller ("prophet"); by analogy, an inspired speaker; by extension, a poet
25.dv: from a compound of G4253 (πρό) and G5346 (φημί);

25.gl: something said (including the thought); by implication, a topic (subject of discourse), also reasoning (the mental faculty) or motive; by extension, a computation; specially, (with the article in John) the Divine Expres
25.kj: account, cause, communication, X concerning, doctrine, fame, X have to do, intent, matter, mouth, preaching, question, reason, + reckon, remove, say(-ing), shew, X speaker, speech,

27.gl: the heart, i.e. (figuratively) the thoughts or feelings (mind); also (by analogy) the middle

28.gl: figuratively, a magistrate; by Hebraism, very
28.dv: of uncertain affinity; a deity, especially (with G3588 (ὁ)) the supreme Divinity;
feminine of a derivative of G4990 (σωτήρ) as (properly, abstract) noun;

figuratively, a magistrate; by Hebraism, very
